question: Was his job title a nurse, assistant, or something else?
answer:
I personally gave a pint of blood while I was doing surgery on him because he had a big bleed in the intestinal area; one of the big arteries was ruptured. 

While I was doing the surgery, I held the bleeding vessel with my right hand and told the nurse (who was a male nurse), "Unglove me and take a pint of my blood." I told him to hang the blood without a cross-match, and he did. 

After I was finished with the surgery, Peter became conscious. He asked me what had happened, and I said, "I will tell you later, not now."I personally give my own blood without type or cross-match because I am type O, and type O is the universal donor. You can give blood to groups A, B, and AB, but group O people cannot receive any blood except for group O. Even though you can give blood to group A, B, and AB, you can only receive from your own group.
